title: CLIP-Lite: Information Efficient Visual Representation Learning with Language Supervision
authors: Not explicitly mentioned in provided text
abstract: CLIP-Lite is an information-efficient variation of CLIP that works effectively in smaller data regimes, requires fewer negative sample pairs during training, and provides comparable or superior performance on standard benchmarks against other methods trained at the same scale.

methodology.approach: Contrastive learning between vision and language modalities
methodology.base_model: Based on CLIP but with modifications to reduce negative sample requirements
methodology.key_technique: Uses a lower bound based on Jensen-Shannon Divergence to maximize mutual information
methodology.negative_sampling: Requires only one negative example pair for each positive example pair

innovations: Reduces number of negative examples in a training batch from O(n²) (like CLIP) to O(n)
Works effectively in smaller data regimes
Uses Jensen-Shannon Divergence-based lower bound instead of InfoNCE
benchmarks: Standard benchmarks (not specified in provided text)
comparisons: Compared to traditional image classification networks pre-trained on ImageNet
key_advantages: Does not rely on as many negative sample pairs during training
